title: Verified RAG methodology knowledge pack v1
summary: Directly opened user-supplied RAG methodology links and supplemental component docs, then distilled them through 10-agent cross-review into accepted DinoBrain RAG knowledge.
claim: DinoBrain should treat RAG as a source/chunk/claim-provenance loop: ingest and index bounded source units, retrieve with honest sparse+dense or lexical-fallback labeling, rerank bounded candidates, evaluate retrieval and behavior separately, and promote only reviewed claims into memory.
accepted_claims: Core RAG separates ingestion/indexing from query-time retrieval and generation.
RAG improves grounding only when retrieved context is relevant, trustworthy, and authorized; it is not a truth guarantee.
Durable RAG needs source/chunk/claim provenance and citations, not unverified URL anchors.
Chunking is application-specific; long documents should be split into stable retrievable units with parent metadata.
Contextual Retrieval is ingestion-time chunk enrichment and must be locally evaluated before its performance claims are generalized.
Hybrid retrieval should combine real sparse/BM25 and real dense vector retrieval with fusion; lexical-only fallback must be labeled as fallback.
Reranking should run after bounded candidate retrieval/fusion; it improves precision but cannot recover missing candidates.
HyDE and RAPTOR are optional retrieval-support methods; Reflexion is agent feedback memory, not core RAG.
GraphRAG is a graph overlay for multi-hop and cross-document synthesis, not a universal replacement for ordinary retrieval.
Evaluation must separately measure retrieval quality, answer faithfulness/relevance, task success, memory-on/off deltas, and latency.
Managed knowledge-base products are provider adapters with different lifecycle, ACL, citation, region, cost, and observability behavior.
Current OpenAI Retrieval guide is authoritative for OpenAI retrieval behavior; the embeddings QA cookbook is conceptual background.
hold_claims: Lewis RAG paper architecture details are paper-specific historical context.
Anthropic Contextual Retrieval performance deltas are benchmark-specific until DinoBrain canaries reproduce gains.
Cloud provider feature claims can drift and should remain source-bound.
MTEB and STS are embedding priors, not proof of DinoBrain memory-loop quality.
rejected_generalizations: URL anchors alone are verified knowledge.
Lexical fallback is equivalent to dense hybrid retrieval.
More stored memory automatically improves behavior.
HyDE, RAPTOR, Reflexion, GraphRAG, and managed KB products are mandatory core RAG components.

lifecycle_history.reason: Accepted memory failed lifecycle gate: durable_external_provenance_missing, unsupported_pressure
